title: pitcairnia ruderalis
intro: Pitcairnia ruderalis, also known as the wild pineapple or prairie pine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. It is a terrestrial bromeliad that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Pitcairnia ruderalis can be consumed as a tea or used in herbal preparations, such as infusions and decoctions, to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
